purpose.summary: This COBOL program processes authorization requests received from an MQ queue, retrieves related data from CICS files, makes an authorization decision, and sends a response. It extracts request message details, reads card cross-reference, account, customer, authorization summary, and profile data, and logs errors if any issues occur during the process.
purpose.business_context: This program is likely part of a larger transaction processing system for authorizing financial transactions.

inputs.name: W01-GET-BUFFER
inputs.description: Authorization request message received from the MQ queue.

inputs.name: WS-CCXREF-FILE
inputs.description: Card cross-reference file containing card number to account ID mapping.

inputs.name: WS-ACCTFILENAME
inputs.description: Account master file containing account details.

outputs.name: MQ Queue (Reply)
outputs.description: Authorization response message sent back to the requesting application.

outputs.name: Error Log
outputs.description: Log file containing error messages and details.

called_programs.purpose: To read a message from the MQ queue.

business_rules.description: If the card is not found in the cross-reference file, a warning message is logged.
business_rules.logic_summary: Checks WS-RESP-CD after reading the CARD-XREF-RECORD. If DFHRESP(NOTFND), sets CARD-NFOUND-XREF to TRUE and logs a warning.

business_rules.description: If the account is not found in the account master file, a warning message is logged.
business_rules.logic_summary: Checks WS-RESP-CD after reading the ACCOUNT-RECORD. If DFHRESP(NOTFND), sets NFOUND-ACCT-IN-MSTR to TRUE and logs a warning.

transforms.transformation_description: Converts alphanumeric transaction amount to numeric using FUNCTION NUMVAL.

copybooks_used.purpose: Defines the structure of the card cross-reference record.
Defines the structure of the account record.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph extracts data from the authorization request message (W01-GET-BUFFER) received from the MQ queue. It uses the UNSTRING statement to parse the comma-delimited message into individual fields such as authorization date, time, card number, and transaction amount. The alphanumeric transaction amount (WS-TRANSACTION-AMT-AN) is converted to a numeric value (PA-RQ-TRANSACTION-AMT) using the NUMVAL function. Finally, the numeric transaction amount is moved to WS-TRANSACTION-AMT. The extracted fields are stored in the PA-RQ-* and WS-* variables for subsequent processing. No error handling is performed within this paragraph; it assumes the message is correctly formatted.

This paragraph serves as a simple exit point for the 2100-EXTRACT-REQUEST-MSG paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement and does not perform any specific actions or logic. It is used to provide a common point for exiting the paragraph, potentially to simplify control flow or to allow for future expansion without modifying the main logic of the paragraph.

This paragraph reads an authorization request message from the MQ queue. It sets MQGMO options for no syncpoint, waiting for a message, conversion, and failing if quiescing. It then calls the MQGET API to retrieve the message into W01-GET-BUFFER. If the MQGET call is successful (WS-COMPCODE = MQCC-OK), it saves the correlation ID and reply queue name. If the call fails, it checks if the reason is MQRC-NO-MSG-AVAILABLE; if so, it sets NO-MORE-MSG-AVAILABLE to TRUE. Otherwise, it logs an error message with the error location, code, and event key using the 9500-LOG-ERROR paragraph. The paragraph consumes the W01-HCONN-REQUEST and W01-HOBJ-REQUEST as input and produces W01-GET-BUFFER as output.

This paragraph serves as a simple exit point for the 3100-READ-REQUEST-MQ paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement and does not perform any specific actions or logic. It is used to provide a common point for exiting the paragraph, potentially to simplify control flow or to allow for future expansion without modifying the main logic of the paragraph.

This paragraph orchestrates the authorization processing. It first sets APPROVE-AUTH to TRUE. It then performs 1200-SCHEDULE-PSB. It sets CARD-FOUND-XREF and FOUND-ACCT-IN-MSTR to TRUE. It then performs 5100-READ-XREF-RECORD to read the card cross-reference record. If the card is found in the cross-reference (CARD-FOUND-XREF), it performs 5200-READ-ACCT-RECORD, 5300-READ-CUST-RECORD, 5500-READ-AUTH-SUMMRY, and 5600-READ-PROFILE-DATA. Finally, it performs 6000-MAKE-DECISION and 7100-SEND-RESPONSE. If the card was found in the cross-reference, it performs 8000-WRITE-AUTH-TO-DB. This paragraph controls the overall flow of the authorization process, calling various sub-paragraphs to retrieve data and make decisions.

This paragraph serves as a simple exit point for the 5000-PROCESS-AUTH paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement and does not perform any specific actions or logic. It is used to provide a common point for exiting the paragraph, potentially to simplify control flow or to allow for future expansion without modifying the main logic of the paragraph.

This paragraph reads the card cross-reference record from the WS-CCXREF-FILE CICS dataset. It moves the card number (PA-RQ-CARD-NUM) to the XREF-CARD-NUM field, which is used as the RIDFLD for the READ command. The CICS READ command retrieves the CARD-XREF-RECORD. The paragraph then evaluates the WS-RESP-CD to determine the outcome of the READ. If the record is found (DFHRESP(NORMAL)), CARD-FOUND-XREF is set to TRUE. If the record is not found (DFHRESP(NOTFND)), CARD-NFOUND-XREF and NFOUND-ACCT-IN-MSTR are set to TRUE, and a warning message is logged using 9500-LOG-ERROR. If any other error occurs, a critical error message is logged using 9500-LOG-ERROR. The paragraph uses the XREF-CARD-NUM as input and retrieves the CARD-XREF-RECORD as output.

This paragraph serves as a simple exit point for the 5100-READ-XREF-RECORD paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement and does not perform any specific actions or logic. It is used to provide a common point for exiting the paragraph, potentially to simplify control flow or to allow for future expansion without modifying the main logic of the paragraph.

This paragraph reads the account record from the WS-ACCTFILENAME CICS dataset. It moves the account ID (XREF-ACCT-ID) from the cross-reference record to the WS-CARD-RID-ACCT-ID field, which is then moved to WS-CARD-RID-ACCT-ID-X and used as the RIDFLD for the READ command. The CICS READ command retrieves the ACCOUNT-RECORD. The paragraph then evaluates the WS-RESP-CD to determine the outcome of the READ. If the record is found (DFHRESP(NORMAL)), FOUND-ACCT-IN-MSTR is set to TRUE. If the record is not found (DFHRESP(NOTFND)), NFOUND-ACCT-IN-MSTR is set to TRUE, and a warning message is logged using 9500-LOG-ERROR. If any other error occurs, a critical error message is logged using 9500-LOG-ERROR. The paragraph uses the XREF-ACCT-ID as input and retrieves the ACCOUNT-RECORD as output.

This paragraph serves as a simple exit point for the 5200-READ-ACCT-RECORD paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement and does not perform any specific actions or logic. It is used to provide a common point for exiting the paragraph, potentially to simplify control flow or to allow for future expansion without modifying the main logic of the paragraph.

error_handling.condition: WS-COMPCODE not equal to MQCC-OK in 3100-READ-REQUEST-MQ and WS-REASON not equal to MQRC-NO-MSG-AVAILABLE
WS-RESP-CD not equal to DFHRESP(NORMAL) or DFHRESP(NOTFND) in 5100-READ-XREF-RECORD
WS-RESP-CD not equal to DFHRESP(NORMAL) or DFHRESP(NOTFND) in 5200-READ-ACCT-RECORD
